title: using-git-worktrees

goal.text: Ensure work begins in the right isolated workspace by detecting existing isolation first, preferring native tools, and falling back to git safely.

requirements.summary: Use this when feature or implementation work should happen in an isolated workspace instead of the current checkout.
acceptanceCriteria: The workflow detects existing isolation before attempting creation.
Native worktree tooling is preferred over manual git fallback.
Project-local worktree directories are verified ignored before creation.
Setup and clean-baseline checks happen before claiming the workspace is ready.

tasks.title: Detect existing isolation before creating anything
tasks.detail:
Check whether the current checkout is already an isolated linked worktree, apply the submodule guard, and determine whether creation should be skipped entirely.

Use [CLAW-KNOWLEDGE.md](/D:/Users/chany/Documents/claw-kit/packages/codex-adapter/skills/using-git-worktrees/CLAW-KNOWLEDGE.md) for the detection and submodule rules.

already-isolated.summary: An isolated workspace already exists, so creation must be skipped.
nextsteps: Proceed directly to setup and baseline verification.

need-isolated-workspace.summary: This is a normal checkout or submodule context, so isolation creation logic should continue.

tasks.title: Prefer native worktree tools, then decide whether git fallback is allowed
tasks.detail: If a native worktree tool exists, use it and skip manual git worktree creation. Only continue to git fallback when no native tool is available.

nextsteps: Proceed to project setup and baseline verification.

use-git-fallback.summary: No native tool is available, so manual git worktree fallback is required.
nextsteps: Resolve directory priority and ignore verification before creation.

tasks.title: Run git worktree fallback safely when needed
tasks.detail: Choose the directory using source priority rules, verify project-local directories are ignored, and create the git worktree. If creation is blocked by sandbox or permissions, fall back to working in place after setup and baseline checks.

fallback-created.summary: The fallback worktree was created successfully.
nextsteps: Run project setup and clean-baseline verification.

work-in-place-fallback.summary: Worktree creation was blocked, so work must continue in the current checkout.
nextsteps: Still run setup and baseline verification before proceeding.

tasks.title: Run setup, verify baseline, and stop if the baseline is not clean
tasks.detail: Run project-appropriate setup commands, then run the clean-baseline test command. If tests pass, report the workspace ready. If tests fail, report the failures and ask whether to proceed or investigate before continuing feature work.

references.why: Preserves the detection-first workflow, native-tool preference, directory-priority rules, and baseline gating from the source skill.
Keeps the full command blocks, quick reference, and longer warning text beside the converted entry.
Maps source information to the converted homes for verification and maintenance.

rules: Do not create a worktree when Step 0 detection shows the workspace is already isolated.
Do not skip the submodule guard when using git-dir/common-dir detection.
Do not use manual git worktree commands when native worktree tooling is available.
Do not create a project-local worktree without verifying the directory is ignored.
Do not skip setup or baseline test verification.
Do not proceed past failing baseline tests without asking whether to proceed or investigate.
